Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen displays an instructional coding video interface, likely for a tool like p5.js or Processing due to the code editor and canvas setup. The title "Randomness and Noise" is prominently displayed at the top. A green line graph is shown on a black canvas, fluctuating with varying amplitude and frequency; a red horizontal line sits at the bottom of the graph. 

The code editor on the left shows:

The UI includes controls for play/pause, step forward, and a "Stop" button.  There are also icons representing variables or parameters (e.g., a circle with a number "5", a small square). The bottom right corner shows zoom controls (+/-).

Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding environment, likely Processing or p5.js due to the tool icons visible on the left sidebar (A+, A-, etc.). The title "Randomness and Noise for loop" is prominently displayed at the top. A white line graph with jagged peaks and valleys is drawn on a black background, overlaid with a horizontal red line at y=0. Below the graph is handwritten text: "height * noise(x)". 

The left sidebar contains icons for saving, running, and other common coding functions. A small circular green indicator is present near the bottom left of the screen, and a set of control buttons (play, step, stop) are visible at the bottom right.
